Title: Alternative Splicing in the Anthocyanin Fruit Gene Encoding an R2R3 MYB Transcription Factor Affects Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in Tomato Fruits

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in Aft top peel under light; very low in WT fruit peel
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in Aft fruit peel at mature green stage, especially light-exposed top epicarp
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required partner enabling functional SlAN2likeAft to activate late anthocyanin genes and accumulate anthocyanins
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; dual-luciferase transactivation assays; split-luciferase complementation; BiFC
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlAN1 is strongly induced in light-exposed Aft fruit peel but not WT peel. 2. It interacts with SlAN2likeAft, but not the truncated WT SlAN2like, enabling MBW complex formation and activation of anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be paired with functional MYB activators to enhance anthocyanin production
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as inducible bHLH2 in hierarchical MBW cascade.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in both fruit halves, with activity in Aft top peel under light
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as constitutive bHLH1 partner in the first MBW complex; supports SlAN2likeAft but not nonfunctional WT SlAN2like in transactivation assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlJAF13 is constitutively expressed in fruit peel. 2. It can support SlAN2likeAft-mediated activation of SlDFR, but WT SlAN2like remains inactive even with SlJAF13.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful supporting factor when stacking anthocyanin regulators
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions as bHLH1 in the initial MBW complex upstream of SlAN1 induction.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively expressed in both top and bottom halves of WT and Aft fruit peel
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Constitutive WDR component required for anthocyanin-regulatory MBW complex in fruit peel
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; split-luciferase complementation (lack of direct MYB binding tested in this paper)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlAN11 is constitutively expressed in WT and Aft fruit peel. 2. It is part of the MBW framework but does not directly bind SlAN2like in the assay, consistent with WDR-bHLH interaction architecture.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Support component for anthocyanin engineering when combined with MYB/bHLH activators
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly interacting with SlAN2like in split-luc assay, consistent with previous reports.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in Aft top peel and much lower in WT peel
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in Aft fruit peel under light where anthocyanin pathway is active
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Feedback-associated repressor marker of active anthocyanin MBW signaling; higher expression in Aft reflects stronger pathway activation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB-ATV expression is much higher in Aft than WT fruit peel. 2. Its induction is consistent with feedback regulation from the active MBW complex promoting anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to relieve repression when maximizing anthocyanin accumulation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May limit overaccumulation through feedback repression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a pathway repressor measured in Results, but not the main focus gene.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective pigmentation and health-beneficial phytochemicals; likely protection against high light/UV
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Fruit peel/epicarp, especially light-exposed top (stem end) of mature green fruits
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3 MYB; contains MYB DNA-binding domains; functional allele includes R3 domain with bHLH-binding motif [DE]Lx2[RK]x3Lx6Lx3R
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SlAN1; SlDFR; SlANS; anthocyanin late biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Functional Aft allele activates SlAN1 and late biosynthetic genes; WT alternatively spliced forms fail to activate SlDFR due to loss of functional MBW complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional SlAN2likeAft promotes anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit peel under light, whereas WT SlAN2like is largely nonfunctional because of alternative splicing
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis via MBW-complex-dependent transcriptional activation of late biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary R2R3 MYB factor promoting anthocyanin synthesis in Aft fruit peel; key determinant of the Aft purple-fruit phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in MYB-bHLH-WDR (MBW) complexes with SlJAF13/SlAN1 and SlAN11; direct interaction with SlAN1 validated
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Permissive/high light conditions; direct light exposure of fruit peel
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation through MBW complex formation; WT allele impaired by alternative splicing leading to truncated nonfunctional proteins
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in hierarchical activation of SlAN1 and reinforcement of anthocyanin pathway; indirectly linked to feedback induction of repressor SlMYB-ATV via MBW activity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In Aft fruit peel, highly expressed in light-exposed top epicarp at mature green stage; much lower in WT fruit peel
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in Aft top fruit peel under light; very low in WT Ailsa Craig and Heinz 1706 fruit peel
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; protoplast dual-luciferase transactivation assay; Nicotiana benthamiana agroinfiltration; RT-PCR cloning of splice variants; synthetic CDS complementation in transactivation assay; split-luciferase complementation; bimolecular fluorescence complementation; anthocyanin quantification
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlAN2likeAft strongly activates the SlDFR promoter and induces anthocyanin accumulation, while WT SlAN2like does not. 2. SlAN2like is the most highly expressed anthocyanin-promoting R2R3 MYB in Aft fruit peel exposed to light. 3. WT SlAN2like transcripts are alternatively spliced, causing frameshifts and premature truncation that abolish interaction with SlAN1 and MBW function. 4. A synthetic canonically spliced WT CDS restores transactivation ability, demonstrating that aberrant splicing underlies the WT loss of function.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for breeding/engineering purple anthocyanin-rich tomato peel; restoring canonical splicing or introducing functional Aft/S. chilense allele should enhance fruit anthocyanin accumulation under light
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Anthocyanin induction is light dependent and largely restricted to exposed peel, potentially causing nonuniform pigmentation; no yield penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter activity differences did not explain higher Aft expression; the main causative mechanism is alternative splicing of the WT allele. A synthetic canonically spliced WT CDS is functional but somewhat less active than Aft, likely due to C-terminal polymorphisms.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S (constitutive, experimental expression); native SlDFR promoter used as reporter; native SlAN2like promoters tested

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SlDFR promoter similarly to SlAN2likeAft
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional wild-species ortholog supports anthocyanin pathway activation and is consistent with purple-pigmented S. chilense fruits
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Functional ortholog used to support the causal role of SlAN2likeAft-type activity in Aft pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Presumed MBW complex with SlAN1 and SlAN11 in assay context
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Light exposure in source species fruits; experimental expression in tomato protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light-exposed fruits in source species; transactivation assay conditions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ScAN2like activated the SlDFR promoter similarly to SlAN2likeAft. 2. Its functionality supports the idea that the Aft trait derives from a functional S. chilense-like AN2like allele.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful donor allele/source for introgression or transgenic engineering of anthocyanin-rich tomato fruit peel
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as orthologous evidence rather than the central tomato gene under mechanistic dissection.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Late anthocyanin biosynthetic genes; likely SlAN1 in MBW cascade
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Can activate anthocyanin pathway when expressed, but endogenous fruit-peel expression in WT was insufficient to trigger anthocyanin synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Capable activator of anthocyanin biosynthesis, but not the primary determinant of Aft fruit pigmentation in this study
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Secondary/insufficient in fruit peel under tested conditions; background positive regulator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW complex with SlJAF13/SlAN1 and SlAN11 inferred from previous work cited
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Hierarchical activation of anthocyanin pathway inferred
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In WT fruit peel, more expressed than in Aft but still insufficient; higher in top than bottom peel under light
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in fruit peel, but lower than SlAN2like in Aft and insufficient in WT for pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; transactivation assay in tomato protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlAN2 can activate anthocyanin-pathway reporter in protoplast assays. 2. In WT fruit peel, endogenous SlAN2 expression is not sufficient to activate anthocyanin synthesis. 3. SlAN2 is not the major causal regulator of the Aft fruit phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Overexpression can be used to engineer anthocyanin-rich tomato fruit, but native fruit-peel expression is limiting
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires sufficiently high expression to be effective; no specific tradeoff reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally compared in Results, though the paper concludes SlAN2like is primary.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin pathway reporter in transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional activator in assays, but transcript levels were barely detectable in fruit peel and thus not a major contributor to Aft fruit pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not decisive in fruit peel under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Barely detectable in Aft and WT fruit peel at mature green stage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; transactivation assay in tomato protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlANT1 from WT and Aft strongly transactivated the SlDFR promoter in protoplasts. 2. Its expression in fruit peel was negligible, indicating little contribution to the Aft phenotype under the tested conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for transgenic anthocyanin biofortification when overexpressed
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Native peel expression is too low for effect; no specific tradeoff reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional competence does not equate to physiological relevance in Aft fruit peel.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin pathway reporter in transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional activator in assays, but weakly expressed in fruit peel and therefore not a major determinant of the Aft phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not decisive in fruit peel under tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Barely detectable in Aft and WT fruit peel at mature green stage
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; transactivation assay in tomato protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlANT1like from WT and Aft strongly transactivated the SlDFR promoter in protoplasts. 2. Its fruit-peel transcript levels were minimal, supporting a negligible role in Aft pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target if ectopically expressed
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Native expression too low for physiological effect; no specific tradeoff reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Assay-active but not physiologically prominent in fruit peel.
